title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description:
Given a binary tree represented as `[val, left, right]`, return its **level order traversal** as an array of arrays, where each inner array contains the values at that level.

Use `null` for missing children. Use `null` as the root for an empty tree.

**Example:**
```
root = [3, [9, null, null], [20, [15, null, null], [7, null, null]]]
Result = [[3], [9, 20], [15, 7]]
```
hints: BFS processes nodes level by level — perfect for this.
Use an array as a queue. Process all nodes at the current level before moving to the next.
Track the level size at the start of each iteration.
